title: mesua planigemma
intro: Mesua planigemma, also known as the horse-sugar tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand, and is classified as a member of the family Melastomataceae. The plant's leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine, known for their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The medicinal properties of Mesua planigemma are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for medicinal purposes.
